summary: Broad item decomp/matching pass across 25 files. The PR body only states progress counters, but the bot report gives strong evidence of impact: +14,840 matched-code bytes, +15,092 linked-code bytes, 32 new matches, and 34 improvements in unmatched items. The biggest milestone was promoting `src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c` from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`; many other item, item collision, data-symbol, and header layout tweaks improved remaining item work.
key_files: configure.py - flipped `melee/it/items/itbombhei.c` from NonMatching to Matching
config/GALE01/symbols.txt - adjusted local/global scopes, rodata sizes, sdata/sdata2 local constants, and split/renamed item data symbols
src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c - major matching cleanup plus state table/data and helper inlines; bot reported object/data and several functions newly matched
src/melee/it/it_266F.c - RandomItemSpawner pointer-aliasing, call-order, and local-scope changes for item spawn/matching functions
src/melee/it/it_2725.c - matched several shared item functions with callback signature, stack aliasing, volatile argument, command-union, and Pokemon selection changes
src/melee/it/it_2725.h - updated `fn_80277D8C` prototype to take three arguments
src/melee/it/itCharItems.h - added `AnimBundle` and used it in `itLinkBoomerangAttributes`
src/melee/it/itcoll.c - added item-specific collision dynamics descriptor structs and refined collision/dynamics loops
src/melee/it/items/itdosei.c - rotation/assert helper restructuring and data-order changes; bot reported `.data` and several motions reaching 100%
src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c - combined float data into `it_803F6A84`, removed old extern, and tuned stack/register behavior in arrow anim code
src/melee/it/items/itlinkboomerang.c - reused `AnimBundle`, factored boomerang helper logic, and adjusted stack layout for quaternion/rotation work
src/melee/it/items/itlinkhookshot.c - extensive ItemLink/chain physics matching, bitfield access changes, stack padding, and no-inline wrapper use
src/melee/it/items/itsamusmissile.c - introduced missile clamp and rotation helper inlines; bot reported `itSamusmissile_UnkMotion0_Phys` and shield-bounce match gains
src/melee/it/items/itseakchain.c - helper loaders/clamps, `#pragma dont_inline`, and stack layout changes for chain physics
src/melee/it/items/itmewtwoshadowball.c - include/data cleanup and stack/local refactors; bot reported new matches for anim and helper functions
what_changed: `configure.py` now treats `src/melee/it/items/itbombhei.c` as `Object(Matching, ...)`, making Bombhei the clear TU-level milestone of the PR.
The PR body recorded remaining item work as `Remaining item TUs: 20` and `Remaining item functions: 90`.

decomp.dev reported 32 new matches, including `itbombhei` `.data`, `fn_8028007C`, `fn_80280974`, `itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Anim`, `itBombhei_UnkMotion2_Coll`, `itBombhei_UnkMotion4_Anim`, `itdosei` `.data`, `itlinkbomb` `.rodata`, `it_266F:it_8026D3CC`, and several small `it_2725` functions.

decomp.dev also reported 34 improvements in unmatched item work, prominently `itseakchain:it_802BC080`, `itlinkboomerang:it_802A0534`, `itsamusgrapple:it_802B75FC`, `itdosei:itDosei_UnkMotion4_Anim`, `itcoll:it_80270E30`, and `itlinkhookshot:it_802A5770`.

`symbols.txt` changed several item constants from named globals to compiler-local `@NNN` labels, corrected zero-Vec3 rodata sizes from `0x10` to `0xC`, marked assert strings local/static, and split the Link arrow data around `lbl_803F6A78` and `it_803F6A84`.
`itCharItems.h` added `AnimBundle { HSD_AnimJoint*, HSD_MatAnimJoint*, HSD_ShapeAnimJoint* }` and `itlinkboomerang.c` switched from a local casted struct to the shared typed bundle.
`itcoll.c` introduced `ItCollDynamicsDesc` and `ItCollDynamics`, then cast `article->x14_dynamics` to the item-specific layout instead of using the previous generic dynamics descriptor access.

`it_2725.c` made `it_803F5440` static, changed `fn_80277D8C` to a 3-argument callback assigned with an `HSD_MObjSetupFunc` cast, used stack aliasing for a `GXColor`, cached `cmd->u` as `union CmdUnion*`, and made `it_8027B288` take a volatile `u32`.

Many item files were retuned with small helper inlines or extracted helpers, including `fn_80280974_inline`, `itBombhei_UnkMotion1_Setup`, `itSamusMissile_ClampTurn`, `it_802BF4A0_adjust_tail`, `itSeakChain_clamp_x10`, and `it_802A6A78_normalize_diff`.

Several changes were purely compiler-shape oriented: declaration reordering, `PAD_STACK`, explicit `_pad` arrays, stack wrapper structs, `register` locals, `#pragma dont_inline`, and deliberate no-op branches labelled in-code as `permuterslop`.
smart_moves: The PR did not stop at function bodies; it updated build classification and symbol metadata once Bombhei became matching, which keeps project progress accounting honest.

Promoting repeated data patterns into real types was a strong header/layout move: `AnimBundle` made Link boomerang animation/matanim/shapeanim triples explicit, and `ItCollDynamicsDesc` made item collision dynamics access more direct.
The matching work repeatedly isolated compiler-sensitive snippets into `static inline` helpers instead of duplicating large blocks, making register allocation and inlining behavior easier to control.

Data-symbol hygiene was treated as part of matching: assert strings and literal constants were marked local/static where the compiler emitted locals, and zero vectors were given the correct `0xC` object size.

For final-byte matches, the PR used targeted stack and ABI controls rather than broad rewrites: `volatile u32` in `it_8027B288`, callback prototype/cast correction for `fn_80277D8C`, stack pointer aliasing in `it_80278108`, and `#pragma dont_inline` around `it_802BBAEC`.

The Bombhei cleanup is reusable because it separated state setup, velocity reset, and boundary checks into named helpers while preserving item-state behavior and enabling the TU to be marked Matching.
decomp_lessons: When an item object file reaches full match, update both the source and `configure.py`; the build config is part of the decomp state.
Do not assume every address-named data symbol is global. Compiler-emitted assert strings and constants often need local `@NNN` labels and static C storage to match data layout.
For item attribute blobs, look for repeated adjacent pointer triples or descriptor records. Turning them into small structs can remove casts and improve both readability and codegen.
Declaration order, local scope, volatile qualifiers, and explicit temporary pointers can be decisive for item matching even when semantics are unchanged.
Stack layout should be controlled narrowly with `PAD_STACK`, `_pad` arrays, stack wrapper structs, or pointer aliases when matching evidence demands it.
Callback signatures must match the engine ABI, not just current call sites; `fn_80277D8C` needed the third unused argument and a setup-function cast.
For chain/link item physics, extracting repeated vector normalization, tail adjustment, clamp, and joint-load logic can improve matching while documenting the algorithm.
The PR contains no explanatory human review discussion, so intent beyond matching/progress metrics is inferred from diffs and decomp.dev output rather than explicit author commentary.
naming_conventions: Existing item function patterns were preserved: `it<Item>_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll`, `it_2725_LogicXX_*`, and address-based `it_80......` names where no semantic name was established.
Compiler-shaping helpers often used the target-function prefix plus an `_inline` suffix, for example `fn_80280974_inline`, `it_802A013C_inline`, and `it_802A6A78_normalize_diff`.
Behavioral helpers used concise verb/object names when the purpose was clear, such as `itBombhei_UnkMotion1_Setup`, `itSamusMissile_ClampTurn`, `itSeakChain_clamp_x10`, and `it_802BF4A0_adjust_tail`.
New shared structs used descriptive type names rather than address names: `AnimBundle`, `ItCollDynamicsDesc`, and `ItCollDynamics`.

Data symbols stayed address-based unless the diff had stronger layout evidence; local compiler constants in `symbols.txt` used `@NNN`, while the Link arrow split used `lbl_803F6A78` for a string and `it_803F6A84` for floats.
The PR normalized some generic argument names toward `gobj`, `ip`, `item`, `attr`, `attrs`, or `sa`, matching common item-code naming style.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: `PAD_STACK`, explicit `_pad` byte arrays, and stack wrapper structs were used to reproduce frame layout in hookshot, boomerang, seakchain, linkarrow, missile, and `it_2725` functions.
`it_8027B288` changed `arg1` to `volatile u32` and assigned it into `union Struct2070`, a direct tactic for matching load/store behavior.
`it_8026CB9C` used pointer-to-field aliases like `*(item_kinds = &spawner->x4.x4) = HSD_MemAlloc(...)` to match RandomItemSpawner allocation codegen.
`fn_80277D8C` was changed to the callback ABI `HSD_MObj*, u32, u32` and assigned through `(HSD_MObjSetupFunc)`.
`it_80278108` wrote a `GXColor` through `(GXColor*) ((u8*) &sp1C - 4)` to match stack placement before calling `HSD_TExpSetReg`.
`it_802790C0` introduced a local `union CmdUnion* u = cmd->u` before reading hitbox fields, a small register/codegen nudge.
`itseakchain.c` wrapped `it_802BBAEC` with `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop` to force a wrapper call shape.
`itarwinglaser.c` used a bitwise copy `*(u32*) &spawn.prev_pos.y = *(u32*) &sp28.y`, indicating exact float/word movement mattered.
`itlinkarrow.c` used a dummy zero loop with `UNUSED unsigned char _[8]`, `register Item* ip`, and `return zero` to steer stack/register output.
Some files retained or added deliberate no-op branches labelled `permuterslop` in the diff, showing localized compiler-shape nudges were accepted for remaining matches.
review_feedback: Human review feedback in the slice is minimal: jurrejelle approved the PR on 2026-05-25T05:12:17Z with an empty review body.
There were no line review comments and no requested changes in the available PR dump.
The only substantive comment was the decomp.dev bot report with match/link metrics and lists of new matches/improvements.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for completion of the remaining item TUs/functions after this PR's `Remaining item TUs: 20` and `Remaining item functions: 90` baseline.
Look for other item attribute structs that contain animation/matanim/shapeanim triples and may benefit from `AnimBundle`-style typing.
Audit ItemLink bitfield names around hookshot/chain users after the `x2C_b5/b6/b7` to `x2C_b0/b1/b2` access changes.
Review nearby `symbols.txt` item constants to ensure local compiler strings/floats/doubles are not incorrectly left as global `it_...` symbols.
Use Bombhei as a template for moving a nearly matched item TU to Matching: state table placement, helper inlines, correct data symbols, and configure update.
Check whether the `ItCollDynamicsDesc` layout should replace generic dynamics descriptor access in other item collision paths.
